title: Fermented Dense Polish Plum Tomatoes Stew
description: A hearty, fermented tomato stew packed with deep umami flavors, made from plum tomatoes and simmered to a velvety consistency.
instructions: Wash and cut plum tomatoes into halves, place them in a shallow bowl, sprinkle 20 g salt over the tomatoes and pour 200 ml water to create a light brine. Cover the bowl with a clean cloth and let the tomatoes ferment at room temperature for 24 hours.
After 24 hours, rinse the tomatoes under cold running water to remove excess salt, then drain them thoroughly.
Heat 2 tbsp olive oil in a medium‑sized pot over medium heat. Add the chopped onion and sauté until translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for another 30 seconds until fragrant.
Place the fermented tomatoes in the pot, add marjoram, smoked paprika, and black pepper. Stir to combine.
Pour in 200 ml vegetable stock (or water if you prefer a lighter stew) and bring the mixture to a gentle boil.
Reduce the heat to low, cover, and simmer for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the stew thickens to a dense, velvety consistency.
Taste and adjust seasoning if needed. Ladle the stew into bowls and serve hot, optionally garnished with fresh herbs or a drizzle of olive oil.
